articles.title: Main Event to Open New Location in Woodbury, Minnesota
articles.summary: Dave & Buster's-owned Main Event Entertainment is expanding with a new family entertainment center in Woodbury, Minnesota. The opening signals continued growth for the Main Event brand under D&B's ownership umbrella. FEC operators should watch how the chain's multi-concept rollout strategy plays out in competitive suburban markets.

articles.title: Big Chicken Partners with Elev8 Fun to Expand Florida Entertainment Footprint
articles.summary: Celebrity chef Shaquille O'Neal's Big Chicken brand is teaming up with Elev8 Fun to bring a combined dining and entertainment concept to Florida. The partnership reflects the growing trend of restaurant brands co-locating with FEC operators to drive traffic and dwell time. FEC operators should note the model as a template for food-and-entertainment hybrid deals.

articles.title: $50M EVO Entertainment Destination Breaks Ground in Downtown Clearwater
articles.summary: A site has been cleared for a $50 million EVO Entertainment venue in downtown Clearwater, Florida, signaling major investment in large-format FEC development. EVO Entertainment is known for combining bowling, movies, gaming, and dining under one roof. The project is a benchmark for urban entertainment destination development in competitive Florida markets.

articles.title: West Des Moines Approves Development Agreement for Grand Experience Entertainment Complex
articles.summary: West Des Moines city officials have approved a development agreement for the Grand Experience, a large-scale entertainment destination project. Municipal approval signals strong local government support for entertainment-driven economic development. FEC developers should monitor this project as an example of public-private collaboration in the Midwest.

articles.title: Round1 Bowling & Arcade Eyes FY2027 Opening for Second Maryland Location
articles.summary: Japanese FEC giant Round1 is planning a second Maryland location, targeting a fiscal year 2027 opening. The expansion continues Round1's aggressive U.S. growth strategy, combining bowling, arcade games, and amusements under one roof. Operators in the Mid-Atlantic region should prepare for increased competition from this well-capitalized chain.

articles.title: Funflation Is Driving Up Indoor Leisure Costs for Consumers
articles.summary: A new report highlights how 'funflation' — the rising cost of entertainment and leisure experiences — is squeezing consumer budgets and reshaping spending habits. Indoor entertainment venues are among the categories seeing notable price increases. FEC operators should be mindful of pricing sensitivity as guests weigh value against discretionary spending.

articles.title: Funflation Spreads Beyond Theme Parks to Gaming and Streaming
articles.summary: Rising leisure costs are now extending into gaming and OTT platforms, suggesting a broader consumer affordability challenge across all entertainment categories. For FEC operators, this signals a need to clearly communicate value propositions to price-sensitive families. Bundling, memberships, and loyalty programs may become increasingly important retention tools.

articles.title: Thrillz Atlanta Indoor Adventure Park Now Open
articles.summary: Thrillz Atlanta, a massive indoor adventure park, has officially opened its doors, adding a major new FEC competitor to the Atlanta market. The venue offers a wide range of active entertainment activities appealing to families and teens. FEC operators in the Southeast should take note of Thrillz's format as the brand continues its national expansion.

articles.title: Altitude Trampoline Park to Open New Location in Litchfield Park, Arizona
articles.summary: Altitude Trampoline Park is expanding into Litchfield Park, Arizona, bringing its active entertainment concept to a growing Phoenix-area suburb. The opening adds to Altitude's national footprint of trampoline and adventure park venues. FEC operators in the Southwest should watch the location's performance as an indicator of demand in suburban Arizona markets.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Opens New Location in Parma, Ohio
articles.summary: Slick City Action Park, known for its slide-based indoor attractions, has opened a new venue in Parma, Ohio near Cleveland. The brand continues its rapid national expansion, targeting markets underserved by large-format indoor entertainment. FEC operators should monitor Slick City as an emerging competitor in the active entertainment segment.

articles.title: Lefty's Alley & Eats Opens Bowling and Arcade Venue in Newark, Delaware
articles.summary: Lefty's Alley & Eats has opened a new combined bowling alley, arcade, and dining concept in Newark, Delaware. The venue represents the growing trend of full-service FECs blending food and beverage with amusement offerings. Independent operators looking to compete with national chains can study this format as a community-anchored alternative.

articles.title: New Family Amusement Center with Restaurant and Bar Opening in Charleston Area
articles.summary: A new family amusement center featuring a full restaurant and bar is set to open in the Charleston, South Carolina area. The venue follows the popular eat-and-play model that has become a dominant format for new FEC openings. Operators in the region should take note as the market grows more competitive.

articles.title: New Sportainment Venue Zamania Opens at Ibn Battuta Mall in Dubai
articles.summary: Zamania by Leo & Loona has launched as a new sportainment venue inside Ibn Battuta Mall in Dubai, combining active play with entertainment in a retail environment. The concept reflects the global trend of malls anchoring entertainment destinations to drive foot traffic. International FEC operators should note Dubai's continued investment in experiential leisure.

articles.title: Legends Entertainment Selects Intercard Kiosks for Springfield Arcade
articles.summary: Legends Entertainment has chosen Intercard's cashless kiosk system for its Springfield arcade operation, joining the growing number of FECs moving away from traditional token and cash systems. Cashless payment and game card technology continues to be a top operational upgrade for arcade and FEC venues. Operators evaluating their payment infrastructure should consider Intercard's growing track record in the sector.

articles.title: Panasonic and Illumibot Launch AI-Powered Projection Mapping Mobile Units
articles.summary: Panasonic Projector & Display Americas has partnered with Illumibot to offer AI-driven projection mapping units that use smartphone cameras and natural language prompts to generate and map visuals onto surfaces. The technology lowers the barrier to entry for venues wanting to add immersive projection experiences without complex setups. FEC operators looking to upgrade or add low-cost immersive environments should explore this emerging solution.

articles.title: InfoComm 2026: AV Industry Shifts Focus to Simpler, Easier-to-Operate Systems
articles.summary: InfoComm 2026 highlighted a major industry trend: audiovisual suppliers are prioritizing ease of deployment and operation over raw performance specs. For FEC operators who rely on AV technology for immersive attractions, simpler systems mean lower installation costs and reduced dependency on specialized technical staff. This shift could significantly benefit smaller and mid-size FEC venues.

articles.title: Boxing Arcade Machines Gaining Traction in Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: Boxing arcade machines are increasingly being adopted by entertainment venues as a high-engagement, skill-based attraction that appeals to a broad demographic. The format offers strong revenue-per-square-foot potential and repeat play incentives. FEC operators looking to diversify their arcade floors should consider the category as consumer interest in active gaming grows.

articles.title: YULONG Launches Commercial Shooting Game Machine Series for FEC and Arcade Markets
articles.summary: YULONG has introduced a new line of commercial shooting game machines designed for immersive interactive entertainment in arcades and FECs. The machines aim to deliver high-engagement experiences that can anchor arcade floors and drive repeat visits. Operators seeking fresh arcade content should evaluate YULONG's new series as part of their equipment refresh strategy.

articles.title: Dark Escape Games Installs 'Clown Around' Attraction
articles.summary: Dark Escape Games has completed an installation of its Clown Around attraction, expanding the operator's portfolio of themed escape and dark ride experiences. The product is designed for FEC and family entertainment venues seeking turnkey horror-comedy attractions. Operators looking to differentiate their venue with a distinctive anchor attraction should look at this format.

articles.title: Fort Worth Amusement Business Closing After 35 Years
articles.summary: A long-running Fort Worth amusement business is permanently shutting its doors after 35 years in operation, marking the end of an era for the local FEC market. The closure is a reminder of the competitive pressures facing independent operators as larger chains expand. Industry observers should note the operational and market factors that contributed to the closure.

articles.title: Tornado Terry's FEC in Texas Shuts Down Permanently
articles.summary: Tornado Terry's, a family entertainment center in Texas, is closing for good after years in operation. The closure highlights the ongoing challenges facing independent FEC operators competing against well-funded chains and evolving consumer expectations. Operators should study closures like this to understand the risk factors threatening legacy venues.

articles.title: Dundee to Get Its First Indoor Crazy Golf Venue as Safari & Co Prepares to Open
articles.summary: Safari & Co is set to open Dundee's first indoor crazy golf venue, tapping into the booming UK market for experiential mini golf. The concept joins a wave of themed indoor golf venues that have proven highly popular across Britain. FEC operators in untapped markets should consider whether themed mini golf could anchor a new entertainment concept.

articles.title: Massanutten Resort Debuts Alpine Coaster with 360-Degree Spirals
articles.summary: Virginia's Massanutten Resort has opened Mountain Mayhem, an alpine coaster spanning 2,156 feet of track with two full 360-degree spirals. Alpine coasters continue to be a high-draw outdoor attraction for resorts and FEC-adjacent venues looking to add thrill rides. Operators at resort destinations should evaluate alpine coasters as a strong revenue-generating outdoor amenity.

articles.title: Spare Time Texas Positions Itself as Affordable Indoor Entertainment Option in Pflugerville
articles.summary: Spare Time Texas is marketing its Pflugerville location as a value-oriented indoor entertainment destination for families, emphasizing affordability amid rising leisure costs. The positioning strategy aligns with growing consumer sensitivity to entertainment spending. FEC operators across the country may find value in revisiting their pricing and value messaging.

articles.title: Developer of Proposed $3B Romulus Sports Complex Charged with Fraud
articles.summary: The developer behind a proposed $3 billion sports and entertainment complex in Romulus, Michigan has been charged with fraud, casting the project's future into doubt. The case is a cautionary tale for municipalities and investors considering large-scale entertainment development deals. FEC industry stakeholders should scrutinize developer credentials and financial transparency in major projects.

articles.title: Megaplex Entertainment Center Planned for St. George, Utah's New Paseo Complex
articles.summary: A Megaplex entertainment center is slated to anchor St. George, Utah's new Paseo mixed-use development alongside retail tenants including Nordstrom Rack. The project reflects the continued appetite for entertainment-anchored mixed-use developments in high-growth Sun Belt markets. FEC operators and developers should note the Paseo model as a template for retail-entertainment co-tenancy.

articles.title: Universal Parks Reports Near-Term Softness Despite Epic Universe Revenue Boost
articles.summary: Comcast's Universal theme parks segment posted a 2.7% revenue increase in Q2, driven largely by Epic Universe's opening, but adjusted EBITDA fell 5.1% due to higher operating costs. The company acknowledged near-term softness while expressing long-term confidence in its parks portfolio. For FEC operators, the results highlight the challenge of balancing new attraction investment against rising operational expenses.

articles.title: Gilroy Gardens Launches Dedicated Outdoor Events Center to Diversify Revenue
articles.summary: Gilroy Gardens Family Theme Park has launched the Hecker Pass Outdoor Events Center, a separately branded events destination with its own website and marketing. The move illustrates how FEC and theme park operators can unlock additional revenue streams by repurposing existing space for private events and group bookings. Operators with underutilized outdoor space should consider a similar branded events strategy.

articles.title: Dollywood Named Among America's Greatest Workplaces for Parents and Families
articles.summary: Dollywood Parks & Resorts has been recognized by Newsweek as one of America's Greatest Workplaces for Parents & Families for 2026. The recognition underscores the importance of family-friendly employment policies in attracting and retaining frontline hospitality workers. FEC operators facing staffing challenges can learn from Dollywood's HR and culture strategies.
